rules.sourceTitle: National Health Commission / SAMR / GB Food Safety Standards
rules.notes: Automated extraction failed: {"error":{"code":503,"message":"This model is currently experiencing high demand. Spikes in demand are usually temporary. Please try again later.","status":"UNAVAILABLE"}}

rules.limit: Verification required via GSFA Online Database
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets conditions for permitted food additives in all foods. The online database provides searchable provisions by additive, functional class, and food category, and is regularly updated.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods...

rules.parameter: Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants (e.g., mycotoxins, metals like arsenic, lead, mercury)
rules.limit: Verification required via Codex maximum levels (MLs) or guideline levels
rules.notes: Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) and guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed to protect human health, keeping levels as low as possible based on scientific evidence. Refer to the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Verification required against Union list in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008
rules.notes: This database serves as a tool to inform about approved food additives and their conditions of use.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.limit: Must comply with food safety criteria as laid down in Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: These criteria define the acceptability of a product or a batch of food applicable to products placed on the market.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.limit: Shelf-life studies (durability studies and challenge tests) must comply with food safety criteria defined in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Technical guidance documents provide detailed information on how to conduct shelf-life studies and evaluate laboratory competence for Listeria monocytogenes in RTE foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Technical guidance document on shelf-life studies for Listeria monocytogenes in RTE foods provides specialised laboratories with detailed and practical information on how to conduct shelf-life studies... in order to comply with the food safety criteria defined in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.

rules.limit: Must comply with process hygiene criteria as laid down in Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: These criteria indicate the correct functioning of the production process.
rules.evidenceQuote: In addition, this Regulation lays down certain process hygiene criteria to indicate the correct functioning of the production process.

rules.foodCategory: Certain live animals and products (e.g., poultry, meat, eggs)
rules.limit: Verification required against specific regulations for special guarantees (e.g., Council Decision 95/410/EC, Decision 2004/235/EC, Decision 2003/644/EC, Regulation (EC) No 1688/2005, Regulation (EC) No 853/2004, Regulation (EC) No 2160/2003)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Special guarantees apply to trade from Sweden, Finland, Norway, and other EU countries with control programmes recognized as equivalent.
rules.evidenceQuote: When Sweden and Finland joined the EU special guarantees on Salmonella were provided as regards trade from other countries of certain live animals and products... The current guarantees are laid down in: Council Decision 95/410/EC... Decision 2004/235/EC... Decision 2003/644/EC... Regulation (EC) No 1688/2005...

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2209:2026 Technical Regulation - Supplementary Sports Foods
rules.notes: This regulation likely includes specific additive limits for supplementary sports foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2209:2026 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الرياضية التكميلية

rules.notes: This regulation likely includes specific contaminant limits for supplementary sports foods.

rules.notes: This regulation covers general labelling requirements for all pre-packaged food products, including seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.parameter: General requirements for supplementary sports foods
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2209:2026 Technical Regulation - Supplementary Sports Foods
rules.notes: This regulation likely includes specific labelling requirements for supplementary sports foods.

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 Technical Regulation - Shelf Life of Food Products - Part 1: Mandatory Shelf Life Periods
rules.notes: This regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for food products, which are often determined by microbiological stability.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 Gulf Standard - Shelf Life of Food Products - Part 2: Optional Shelf Life Periods
rules.notes: This standard specifies optional shelf life periods for food products, which are often determined by microbiological stability.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW)
rules.notes: Automated extraction failed: Expected ':' after property name in JSON at position 1550 (line 30 column 5)

Different limits apply depending on the additive and food product type. Use the 'List of Permitted Food Additives' or 'Food Additives Search tool' and 'Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives' to check.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products. Use the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives to learn more about the different types of additives and the corresponding regulations that impose the limits.

rules.parameter: Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
rules.limit: Not directly added to kueh; allowed in fillings/toppings within limits
rules.notes: Class II chemical preservatives should not be directly added when manufacturing kueh. They can only be directly added, within prescribed regulatory limits, to fillings and toppings.
rules.evidenceQuote: Class II chemical preservatives, such as sorbic acid and benzoic acid, should not be directly added when manufacturing the kueh. Class II chemical preservatives can only be directly added, within the prescribed regulatory limits, to fillings and toppings in kuehs.

rules.notes: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.limit: Technological justification and no health risks
rules.notes: Food additives are allowed when there is a technological justification and they do not present health risks to consumers.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives are allowed when: there is a technological justification they do not present health risks to consumers

rules.notes: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.notes: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are used, the 'Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives' must be used to calculate permitted levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.notes: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.notes: If a food product contains an additive that is not permitted, it must not be imported or sold. An application can be submitted to SFA for evaluation.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Food businesses must be aware of commonly found contaminants in their products and conduct laboratory tests as needed.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food businesses must be aware of the contaminants that are commonly found in their food products and conduct laboratory tests as needed.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents (contaminants) in food and also with the Regulatory Limits for Food Additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food. They must also comply with the Regulatory Limits for Food Additives.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: If laboratory test results indicate contaminants, they must be within specified limits. Refer to relevant documents for Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, and Other incidental constituents.
rules.evidenceQuote: If laboratory test results indicate that there are contaminants in your products, they must be within the specified limits. Use the documents above to assist you in complying with the requirements.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: If food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, they must not be imported or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: Meat and food derived from meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat containing residues of diethylstibestrol, hexoestrol, or dienoestrol is prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol (...) hexoestrol (...) dienoestrol (...)

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: SFA has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, effective from 1 May 2020. These MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020. These MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website at the following weblink.

rules.notes: All ingredients and additives used in prepacked food products for sale in Singapore must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits are detailed in the referenced standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products
rules.notes: Specific limits are detailed in the referenced standard. Relevant if seasoning/snacks contain animal ingredients.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products

rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products (e.g., cuttlefish, squid, octopus, takoyaki, dried shredded squid); Spiral shells and Spiral shell products (e.g., escargot, mussel, clam, oyster, scallop, abalone, scallop sauce); Seeds and seed products (e.g., sunflower seed, melon seed, excluding highly refined/purified sunflower seed oil); Kiwis and kiwi products (e.g., kiwi jam, dried kiwi)
rules.limit: Recommended to be labeled if present as an ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These are recommended labeling allergens.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.limit: Suggested statement: “The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or synonymous terms.
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Suggested for situations where there is a risk of allergen cross-contamination.
rules.evidenceQuote: the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
rules.limit: Required to be labeled if present as an ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These are mandatory labeling allergens.
rules.evidenceQuote: For prepackaged foods, food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific requirements for nutrition claims are detailed in the referenced regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory labeling if the concentration of residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.parameter: γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) produced by microbial fermentation
rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific restrictions and labeling requirements are detailed in the referenced regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
